message:
fix(extensions-js): suppress redundant startup render by clearing needsRender after initial pushFrame

Set needsRender = false immediately after the startup pushFrame() call
in the extension UI template to prevent the poll-timer from enqueuing a
duplicate frame that repeats the initial render state. Without this fix,
custom UI extensions would emit two identical frames on startup — one
from pushFrame() and another from the dirty-render timer.

Also refactor the width-change reflow test to align with the new
startup behavior: remove the initial dirty-rerender branch and assert
that the first setWidget call occurs only after a genuine poll-driven
width change (80->40), not from a redundant startup frame.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
